key_findings: Replay debt is safe to accumulate indefinitely in SQLite — INSERT OR IGNORE idempotency in persistence_gateway.py and MERGE idempotency in both sink workers guarantee zero data loss and zero corruption from any replay volume
The burn-down model identifies ClickHouse's theoretical max drain rate of 360,000 rows/hour (Tier C) — enough to drain 200k events in ~34 minutes with scanner paused

Neo4j halving time at peak 200k debt is ~8h under normal conditions (not 4h like CH) — this is acceptable given graph write overhead; Neo4j go/no-go gate is therefore tighter (G-2: < 5,000 vs G-1: < 10,000)

The WO-00026 adaptive controller's sink_lag_h signal already provides first-line throttling — the replay debt throttle tier policy (Tier A/B/C) works as a second layer, adjusting batch_size and sweep_interval independently of PARALLEL
DLQ rows must NEVER be auto-requeued without root cause diagnosis — NON_RETRYABLE errors (auth failure, schema mismatch) will loop infinitely if re-enqueued without a fix
Go/no-go gate G-6 (burn_rate_positive) is the most critical gate for new module activation — if sync_rate_h <= ingest_rate_h, enabling a new scan module guarantees unbounded debt growth

decisions.decision: 4-tier throttle policy (A/B/C/D) rather than continuous rate limiting
decisions.rationale: Discrete tiers are operator-interpretable and easily implemented as env-var overrides to batch_size and sweep_interval; continuous rate limiting requires a separate controller process

decisions.decision: Debt halving time target: 4h for CH, 8h for Neo4j
decisions.rationale: CH ReplacingMergeTree absorbs inserts unconditionally at 60k rows/h; Neo4j graph MERGE is 3x heavier — setting 8h target for Neo4j is realistic without requiring infrastructure changes

decisions.decision: 6 go/no-go gates (G-1 to G-6) with explicit thresholds before new scan module activation
decisions.rationale: Enabling a new scan module increases ingest_rate_h; without gates, a strained system could reach ∞ halving time (debt grows faster than drained); gates ensure headroom before adding load

decisions.decision: DLQ re-enqueue is a manual operation (SQL UPDATE) not an automatic mechanism
decisions.rationale: Automatic re-enqueue of DEAD rows after a timeout would create retry storms for NON_RETRYABLE errors; operator must confirm root cause is fixed before re-enqueue

decisions.decision: Hourly monitoring cadence with 5 key metrics per sink
decisions.rationale: 300s refresh matches WO-00026 controller cadence; hourly model is sufficient for operational visibility; sub-minute monitoring not needed for a batch sync worker

go_no_go_gates.blocks: any new event kind in sink_outbox

data_safety_guarantees: INSERT OR IGNORE in persistence_gateway.py: events stored exactly once in SQLite regardless of replay count
ReplacingMergeTree in ClickHouse: duplicate inserts accepted, server-side dedup on merge
MERGE patterns in Neo4j: idempotent at 10M+ node scale, safe for unlimited replay
CLAIMED reaper (600s TTL): crashed worker rows return to PENDING, never lost

risk_model.risk: DLQ auto-requeue re-creates retry storm for NON_RETRYABLE errors
risk_model.mitigation: Re-enqueue is exclusively manual SQL UPDATE; playbook explicitly requires root cause diagnosis before re-enqueue

risk_model.risk: Tier C bulk burn-down creates SQLite write contention with live scanner
risk_model.mitigation: Tier C is only applied when scanner is paused; WAL mode + BEGIN IMMEDIATE serializes writers safely

risk_model.risk: G-6 not checked before new module activation — debt grows unboundedly
risk_model.mitigation: Go/no-go checklist in playbook STEP 5 must be completed before any module activation; G-6 is the first gate evaluated

risk_model.risk: halving_time = ∞ not detected until after 2+ hours of growth
risk_model.mitigation: Hourly monitoring query (STEP 4) explicitly computes halving time and flags ∞; escalation threshold triggers at 2 consecutive hours

cross_wo_dependencies.WO-00022: chunk_queue backlog drain; scanner PARALLEL setting
cross_wo_dependencies.WO-00023: sink_outbox, event_sync_ledger, dead_letter_events schema; retry taxonomy
cross_wo_dependencies.WO-00025: FM-08 (CH outage) + FM-15 (Neo4j outage) drills validate playbook STEP 1-2
cross_wo_dependencies.WO-00026: Adaptive controller provides first-line throttle via sink_lag_h; Tier B/C augments this
cross_wo_dependencies.WO-00028: CH sink worker batch_size and sweep_interval settings referenced in throttle tiers
cross_wo_dependencies.WO-00029: Neo4j sink worker batch_size and sweep_interval settings referenced in throttle tiers
